rationale: Ground truth is a trivial 'lgtm' approval with no substantive concerns, so both candidates correctly identify this as a clean, upstream-aligned rename with no missed occurrences — both verified against the actual vllm checkout, so recall and grounding are strong for each. X's APPROVE verdict matches the real outcome and its findings (table scan, missed-occurrence grep, constructor-untouched check, mock-limitation note) are all accurate and grounded with no fabrication. Y's sweep is equally accurate but its verdict of COMMENT plus a speculative suggestion to add a comment/test at the getattr sites is a lower-value, non-essential ask that diverges from what actually happened (plain approval), slightly hurting precision despite being more 'actionable' in form.
